segments.text: Puma once created this perfect shoe box.
Absolute genius. It won every major design award and saved millions on fuel.
But why did it completely vanish? For decades, shoe boxes were a disaster.
Heavy [music] cardboard, useless tissue paper, and massive waste. A problem nobody could fix. Then Puma dropped this clever little bag. [music] It cut
cardboard by 65% and replace the heavy lid with a reusable tote. Ultimate win for the planet. But how do you ship a
soft box without crushing the expensive shoes? The designers were brilliant.
They engineered a structural cardboard spine supporting the weight, lighter, smaller, and saved Puma 500,000 [music]
liter of fuel every year. So why did Puma quietly kill it? Because in the real world, it was a nightmare. The red bags gathered dust in warehouses. And
without a solid list, shipping pallets became unstable and hard to stack. Seems like that boring box exists for a reason. For more design tickets secrets,
